rulings.Case Title: KERLINE ASTRE VS. NICOLE MEREDITH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Modify Stay And Seal Record With Court Reporter Fee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 24, 2021, Line 2 PLAINTIFF KERLINE ASTRE'S Motion To Modify Stay And Seal Record With Court Reporter Fee Plaintiff Kerline Astre's motion to modify or impose stay is denied. Plaintiff's motion to seal the declaration of counsel filed on April 28, 2021 is granted as unopposed, although the declaration consists largely of legal argument and the Court does not believe that it meets the standards set forth in Cal. R. Ct. 2.550. *The Court's full and complete tentative ruling has been emailed to all counsel* As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ABRAHAM DEGALA VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion For New Trial (Cahill)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 24, 2021, Line 7 PLAINTIFF ABRAHAM DEGALA'S Motion For New Trial (Cahill) Plaintiff's motion for new trial is denied. A plaintiff may seek a new trial after a court erroneously grants summary judgment against it. (See Collins v. Sutter Mem'l Hosp. (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1, 17-18 [proper to seek new trial based on erroneous grant of motion for summary judgment per Code Civ. Proc. 657(7)].) *The Court's full and complete tentative ruling has been emailed to all counsel* As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO PANIAGUA ET AL VS. MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees On Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 24, 2021, Line 3 PLAINTIFFS EDUARDO PANIAGUA, ELENA ASTURIAS' Motion For Attorneys Fees On Appeal Plaintiffs' motion for attorney's fees on appeal is denied. The general rule is that "fees under [Civil Code] section 1717 are awarded to the party who prevailed on the contract overall, not to a party who prevailed only at an interim procedural step." (DisputeSuite.com, LLC v. Scoreinc.com (2017) 2 Cal.5th 968, 977; accord, City of West Hollywood v. Khiagi (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 739, 753.) In DisputeSuite.com, the California Supreme Court discussed with approval several cases in which "a party has won a procedural victory, ending the contract litigation in one court but the litigation has continued or could continue in a different forum." (Id. at 975-977.) Those cases stand for the principle that "[a] party does not become the prevailing party under the statute merely by obtaining a forum for resolution of the contractual dispute or by moving it from one forum to another." (Id. at 976.) That principle is controlling here. Plaintiffs misread the prevailing party fees clause of the Payoff Agreement. A petition to compel arbitration is not a "provisional remedy in aid of arbitration." (See Code Civ. Proc. 1281.8 [defining provisional remedies]. Unlike the fees clause in Acosta v. Kerrigan (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 1124, the fees clause in the Payoff Agreement does not authorize an interim award of attorney's fees in connection with plaintiffs' successful opposition to defendants' motion to compel arbitration. (See Frog Creek Partners, LLC v. Vance Brown, Inc. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 515, 525.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW LYNCH VS. ALVIN GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 24, 2021, Line 4 PLAINTIFF ANDREW LYNCH'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint Plaintiff's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. The motion is supported by the strong policy allowing liberal amendment of pleadings. Defendants do not contend that they would be prejudiced by the amendment or that plaintiff delayed unduly in seeking leave to amend. To the extent that defendants contend that plaintiff lacks standing to assert derivative claims or that the proposed amendment is otherwise subject to challenge, they may raise those arguments on demurrer or other appropriate motion. (See, e.g., California Casualty Gen. Ins. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].) Defendants are reminded that before filing a demurrer, they are required to meet and confer with plaintiff's counsel. (Code Civ. Proc. 430.41.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
